cards.title: When Your Adult Child Doesn't Want Your Company
cards.body: A parent struggles with their adult son's reluctance to spend time together despite weekly contact. An advice columnist explores whether unspoken pressure might be affecting the relationship.

cards.title: Parenting Anxiety on Family Holiday
cards.body: A parent learns to manage worry during family time and discovers resilience when a real emergency strikes. The experience shifts perspective on what it means to truly be present with children.

cards.title: How Childhood Trauma Affects Adult Relationships
cards.body: A man with narcissistic and avoidant parents discovers his childhood patterns repeating in his own breakup. Learning what healthy relationships look like requires addressing deeply ingrained dysfunctional patterns.

cards.title: Parenting Advice That Backfires
cards.body: A child learns their father's well-intentioned guidance consistently leads them away from their authentic self. Understanding when to accept parental advice and when to chart your own course matters.

cards.title: Only Children Aren't Weird or Selfish
cards.body: Parents of only children face persistent myths about their parenting choices and their children's social development. The reality shows only children thrive and parents aren't being selfish.

cards.title: UK Passport Refusal for Child Raises Questions
cards.body: A British parent living abroad encountered unexpected difficulty getting a passport for their youngest child despite identical documents working for the other two. The inconsistent process raises concerns about fairness.

cards.title: Blended Family Boundaries After Parental Separation
cards.body: Adult children navigate whether to include their father's new partner in family gatherings after he leaves their mother. Balancing fairness with relationship preservation requires difficult choices.

cards.title: You Can Talk to Children About Anything
cards.body: Parents often avoid difficult conversations with their kids unnecessarily. Open dialogue about complex topics strengthens parent-child relationships and helps children develop emotional resilience.

cards.title: Stage Fright in Young Musicians
cards.body: Children experiencing performance anxiety often internalize a critical parent voice that undermines confidence. Breaking this cycle requires addressing the inner critic through honest self-reflection.

cards.title: Building Self-Esteem in Children
cards.body: Parents prioritize self-esteem because it helps children navigate an increasingly demanding world. A strong sense of self provides crucial protection as kids face mounting social and personal pressures.

cards.title: Mental Health Crisis Affecting Million Children
cards.body: Over one million children in England are referred for mental healthcare annually, with anxiety as the leading concern. Demand far exceeds capacity, leaving many young people waiting years for support.
